completionNotes: Completed as a short visible multi-choice checkbox group pattern with source-backed grouping guidance, native checkbox live behavior, radio and multi-select distinctions, duplicate audit, and screenshot spot-check evidence.

completionEvidence.patternSpecificGuidance: Guidance names independent multi-choice options, fieldset and legend grouping, select-all-that-apply hinting, label click targets, group-level validation, preserved checked values, and Space-key toggling.
completionEvidence.relatedPatternDistinction: The entry separates checkbox groups from radio groups by allowing several selected values, and from multi-select by keeping a short independent option set visible instead of using a compact picker.
completionEvidence.liveDemoBehavior: The live demo uses native checkboxes, allows multiple alert channels to be checked independently, preserves the selected set, validates when none are selected, and supports select-all and clear actions.
completionEvidence.comparisonDecisionRules: The linked checkbox-group-vs-multi-select comparison distinguishes visible short-list independent choices from long or searchable multi-value picker behavior.
completionEvidence.duplicateAudit: npm run audit:completion compares normalized guidance fields across patterns and currently reports no duplicate field group blocking this completed pattern.
completionEvidence.manualSpotCheck: screenshots/patterns/checkbox-group.png was inspected after rendering and shows Good UI, Bad UI, Good UX, Bad UX, native checkbox labels, multiple checked values, clear/select-all actions, and group validation behavior.

name: Checkbox group
problem: Users need to choose zero, one, or several independent options from a short set that should remain visible while they decide.
solution: Show native checkboxes in a labeled group with a select-all-that-apply hint, independent checked states, clickable labels, and group validation when a minimum or limit applies.
problemContext: Users need to review all available options and the selected set before submitting.
selectionRules: Choose checkboxes for short independent multi-choice sets where every option can remain visible.
State any minimum, maximum, or select-all-that-apply rule before users submit.
Use radio groups instead when exactly one option is allowed.
Use multi-select when the option set is too long, searchable, or dynamic to show as a visible checklist.
requiredStates: One or more checked option states.
Group validation state when required, limited, or left empty.
interactionContract: Each option toggles independently without unchecking other selected options.
The selected set remains visible in the list and in any summary text.
Validation explains required, minimum, or maximum selection rules without clearing checked values.
implementationChecklist: Use native checkbox inputs where possible.
Group related choices under a clear fieldset legend or equivalent accessible name.
Add a short hint such as select all that apply when the multi-choice rule may be missed.
Avoid hidden defaults, ambiguous negative labels, and custom toggles without checkbox semantics.
commonMisuses: Using checkboxes for mutually exclusive choices.
Not explaining max-selection limits until submit.
Scattering related checkboxes without a shared question or legend.
critiqueQuestions: Are these choices truly independent, and should users see them all together?
Can the user tell how many options may be selected before they submit?
useWhen: Users can choose zero, one, or many options.
The set is short enough to scan and compare without search.
avoidWhen: Only one option can be selected.
The option set is long, dynamic, or needs search.
The selected values need compact chip management or async loading.
accessibility: Use programmatic labels for every checkbox.
Group related checkboxes with a fieldset and legend or equivalent.
Associate group hints and errors through descriptions when available.
keyboardBehavior: Space toggles the focused checkbox without changing other checked options.
failureModes: Losing selected values after submit failure.
Using a checkbox group where a compact searchable multi-select is needed.

sources.note: GOV.UK checkbox guidance supports one-or-more selections, fieldset/legend grouping, select-all-that-apply hints, errors, and independent checked values.
USWDS checkbox guidance supports multiple answers, selectable labels, vertical lists, positive labels, touch spacing, and fieldset/legend grouping.
APG checkbox guidance documents checked, unchecked, and mixed states, accessible labels, grouped descriptions, and Space-key toggling.

uiGuidance: Render visible native checkboxes with labels, checked states, group legend, select-all-that-apply hint, and group validation state.
Keep all short-list independent options visible for comparison and review.
uxGuidance: Let users choose independent options and review the selected set before submitting.
Communicate minimum, maximum, required, or select-all-that-apply rules before errors occur.

good: Visible checkboxes with large hit areas, persistent labels, group legend, and aligned helper text.
Checked, unchecked, focus, disabled, and error states are visually distinct without relying only on color.
bad: Tiny custom boxes that are hard to target.
Checkboxes scattered without a group label or consistent spacing.

examples.notes: Documents one-or-more checkbox groups, fieldset and legend grouping, select-all-that-apply hints, group errors, and independent checked values.

examples.product: U.S. Web Design System Checkbox component
examples.notes: Documents multiple-answer checkbox groups, selectable labels, vertical layout, positive labels, touch targets, and fieldset/legend accessibility.
Documents checked states, accessible labels, grouped descriptions, and keyboard toggling expectations.

good: Clicking the label toggles the checkbox.
Users can select multiple independent choices and review the selected set before submit.
bad: Using checkboxes for mutually exclusive choices.
Losing checked values after validation or page refresh.
